steps.title: Use the graph shortcut
steps.content: For a graphed system of linear equations, the solution is the point where the two lines intersect. You do not need to solve algebraically if the graph is readable.

steps.title: Read the intersection
steps.content: The two lines cross at $x=2$ and $y=4$, so the solution is $(2,4)$.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content: The other listed points lie on at most one of the lines. The solution must lie on both lines at the same time, so the intersection point is the only valid choice.

steps.title: Answer
steps.content: $(2,4)$ matches choice B, so B is correct.
